title: alchemilla monticola var. subpastoralis
intro: Alchemilla monticola var. subpastoralis, also known as the alpine lady's mantle, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the alchemilla monticola plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant is valued for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of alchemilla monticola var. subpastoralis include soothing and protecting the skin, reducing inflammation, and potentially alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
